site.tagline: A people-powered, single-issue campaign building a public mandate for affordable and reliable energy for every Australian household.
site.authorisation: Authorised by Z. Hilton, Coalition for Conservation, Sydney NSW

hero.sub: Sign the petition. Tell Australia's leaders to put families first — before ideology, before politics, before experiments.

voices.name: Janelle, 62
voices.quote: I had to choose between heating and my heart medication this winter. I never thought I'd see that day in Australia.

voices.name: Tom, 47
voices.quote: Our power bill tripled. We cut staff hours, then we cut staff. Nobody in Canberra seems to notice the small shops closing.

voices.name: Priya, 34
voices.quote: Two kids, two jobs, and we still get the disconnection notices. I signed because someone has to say enough.

news.head: Grid stability fears mount as wholesale prices spike across NEM
news.summary: AEMO data reveals wholesale spot prices in NSW and Victoria reached new peaks during the Easter cold snap.
news.body:
AEMO has confirmed that wholesale electricity spot prices in NSW and Victoria touched new highs through the Easter long weekend, with prices repeatedly testing the administered cap. The body has urged renewed work on firming capacity. Affordable Energy Australia notes that every wholesale spike eventually shows up on family bills — and that the relentless drumbeat of new peaks should be a wake-up call for the government to put affordability ahead of ideology.

This is not a fringe issue. It is the defining cost-of-living crisis of our time. The campaign continues to call on Australia's leaders to prioritise affordable and reliable energy.

news.head: 340,000 households now in energy debt — peak body sounds alarm
news.summary: Energy and Water Ombudsman figures show debt levels at a decade high, with average debt up $200 in twelve months.
news.body:
New Energy and Water Ombudsman data shows the number of Australian households carrying energy debt has reached a ten-year high, with average household debt up by more than $200 in the last twelve months. Frontline financial counsellors say they are seeing more families forced to choose between food, healthcare and the power bill.

This is exactly the trajectory the Affordable Energy Australia petition was launched to change. Adding your name puts you on the record demanding that Australia's leaders put affordability first.

news.head: Manufacturers warn of further closures without affordability reset
news.summary: AI Group survey of 600 manufacturers identifies energy as the leading risk to operations through 2027.
news.body:
A new AI Group survey of 600 Australian manufacturers has identified runaway energy costs as the leading operational risk facing the sector through 2027. Several large employers have already wound back production, with the survey suggesting further closures are likely without a deliberate affordability reset.

When manufacturing capacity leaves Australia it does not come back. The petition is the simplest, fastest way Australians can register their support for an energy policy that puts the country's industrial base first.

news.head: AEA petition surpasses 40,000 signatures in cross-electorate movement
news.summary: Affordable Energy Australia confirms supporter base has grown 240% quarter-on-quarter.
news.body:
Affordable Energy Australia has confirmed that its national petition has now surpassed 40,000 signatures, drawn from every state and territory and the overwhelming majority of federal electorates. The supporter base has grown 240% quarter-on-quarter.

The campaign's goal remains 100,000 signatures ahead of the 2028 federal election. If you have not yet added your name, now is the moment. Every signature counts towards the public mandate Australia's leaders cannot afford to ignore.

news.head: Pensioners rationing heating as winter bills bite early
news.summary: COTA reports a sharp rise in members reducing heating use, with health concerns mounting.
news.body:
Council on the Ageing Australia has reported a sharp rise in the number of older Australians actively rationing their heating as energy bills bite early in the colder months. Health professionals warn that cold-related illness in seniors is a documented and accelerating public-health risk in a country where heat and cold exposure together kill more Australians than bushfires.

No Australian pensioner should have to choose between staying warm and paying for groceries. Sign the petition. Make it impossible for any government to ignore.

news.head: AEA names independent advisory panel ahead of 2028 election cycle
news.summary: Five former MPs from a range of backgrounds join the campaign in advisory roles.
news.body:
Affordable Energy Australia has announced an independent advisory panel of five former federal MPs drawn from a range of political backgrounds. The panel will advise on strategy through the 2028 federal election cycle. The campaign remains independent and is not affiliated with any political organisation.

The campaign's mandate continues to grow with every new signature. Add your name to the petition and stand with hundreds of thousands of Australians demanding affordable, reliable energy.

team.name: Daniel Mitchell
team.bio: Former federal political organiser. 15 years across NSW and federal campaigns.

team.bio: Ex-press gallery journalist. Built the comms function for two state-level coalitions.

team.name: Marcus Chen
team.bio: Doorknock veteran. Designs the electorate-by-electorate playbook.

milestones.title: Founding
milestones.desc: AEA incorporated. Founding board appointed. First 1,000 supporters from a standing start.

milestones.title: Petition crosses 50,000
milestones.desc: Surpass 50,000 signatures across all 151 electorates. Public petition handover scheduled.

milestones.title: MP scorecard published
milestones.desc: Every federal MP scored on their public record. Updated quarterly through 2028.

milestones.title: 200,000 supporters
milestones.desc: Half-target supporter list reached. Field organising scaled in 30 priority electorates.

milestones.title: Election cycle
milestones.desc: Cross-electorate field campaign live in 60 marginal seats. Full transparency of funding and impact.
